items.title: 90 Percent of Y Combinator Startups Are Now AI Companies—Here’s Why That’s Alarming
items.description: The program is shorter, startups are pushed to build faster, and just about everyone has an AI company. What could go wrong?

items.title: AI is the New Competitive Career Edge for Entry-Level Job Seekers
items.description: How entry-level employees are automating their own roles to get those precious few jobs.

items.title: How Silicon Valley is Going to War
items.description: A new crop of defense contractors is stepping up as a growing tide of nationalism sweeps Silicon Valley and Washington.

items.title: The Business Case for a Lighter Football Helmet
items.description: As fears over CTE grow, helmet makers are rethinking how much protection is too much. One startup says lighter could actually be safer.

items.title: Inside CoreWeave’s Explosive Marketing Spend—and Why Chance the Rapper Is Now the Star of Its AI Campaign
items.description: Will the AI cloud company’s celeb-studded marketing push pay off?
